stories.headline: Aave-led DeFi rescue tops $300M as industry tries to plug Kelp DAO's $292M hole
stories.tldr: Consensys, Lido, EtherFi and others have pledged into a $300M+ recovery vehicle to backstop Aave bad debt from the April 19 Kelp DAO exploit.
stories.why_it_matters: Why it matters: A coordinated industry bailout — not protocol insurance — is becoming the de facto safety net for cross-chain liquid restaking risk.
the_details: Attacker minted 116,500 unbacked rsETH via a misconfigured LayerZero bridge setting and borrowed ~$230M against it on Aave.
Pledged contributors include Consensys, Lido, EtherFi and Aave-aligned DAOs; most commitments still need governance approval to land.
Researchers attribute the exploit to DPRK-linked Trader Traitor, the same cluster suspected behind Drift's $285M April 1 drain.

stories.headline: 100+ crypto firms hand Senate Banking a deadline: mark up the market structure bill now
stories.tldr: A coalition led by Coinbase and Ripple is publicly pressuring the Senate Banking Committee to schedule a markup of the Clarity Act before recess.
stories.why_it_matters: Why it matters: Without a Senate markup this cycle, the SEC-CFTC March joint release becomes the de facto US framework — leaving structural rules to agency staff, not statute.
the_details: Letter signed by 100+ firms argues the bill is needed to lock in the SEC and CFTC's March 17 token-taxonomy framework.
Lobby push follows the SEC's April 13 staff statement carving a broker-dealer exception for DEX-style user interfaces.
Industry wants statutory clarity ahead of any administration change that could reverse the agencies' joint posture.

stories.headline: SEC's April 13 user-interface carve-out gives DEX front-ends a no-action lane
stories.tldr: SEC staff said it will not object to 'Covered User Interface Providers' offering software that lets users submit crypto-asset-security trades without broker-dealer registration.
stories.why_it_matters: Why it matters: It's the clearest signal yet that interface-only DeFi front-ends can operate onshore — but the conditions attached will define how far that lane really runs.
the_details: Statement issued April 13, 2026 sets conditions on custody, order routing and disclosure for the exception to apply.
Move follows the joint SEC-CFTC interpretive release from March 17 that supersedes the 2019 framework.
Law firms note the carve-out narrows enforcement risk for non-custodial UIs but leaves smart-contract issuers in scope.

stories.headline: Spot SOL ETF inflows collapse 92% from $419M November peak to just $34M in April
stories.tldr: Spot Solana ETF net inflows have fallen from $419M in November 2025 to $34M month-to-date in April as institutional appetite cools.
stories.why_it_matters: Why it matters: The ETF wrapper isn't a one-way bid — and SOL's collapse in flows undercuts the thesis that listed altcoin products would create durable institutional demand.
the_details: SOL trades near $86.47 with a $49.8B market cap, down ~32% from its 2025 YTD high.
Bitcoin dominance is back at 58.1% and the altcoin season index sits at just 33–38/100.
Firedancer rollout and a sub-200ms Alpenglow finality upgrade haven't yet revived ETF demand.

stories.headline: TOKEN2049 Dubai pushed to 2027 as UAE security backdrop reroutes crypto's biggest stage
stories.tldr: Organizers are postponing the Dubai edition of TOKEN2049 to 2027, citing heightened security risks across the UAE region.
stories.why_it_matters: Why it matters: With Iran-Israel tensions still freezing energy and travel risk, the conference circuit — a key liquidity event for deal-making — is being reshuffled around geopolitics.
the_details: TOKEN2049 confirmed the move, pointing to elevated regional security risk in the UAE.
Singapore remains TOKEN2049's flagship event and is unaffected by the rescheduling.
Comes alongside Brent crude above $104 and a closed Strait of Hormuz weighing on risk assets.
